new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a whisky produced by blending one or more single malt Scotch whiskies with one or more single grain Scotch whiskies, all produced in Scotland.
Process of blending one or more single malt Scotch whiskies with one or more single grain Scotch whiskies, carried out in Scotland, to produce Blended Scotch Whisky.

new_classes.label_en: Blended Scotch whisky ('Blended Scotch Whisky'), in containers holding <= 2 l (CN 2208 30 71)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer 'Blended Scotch' Whisky, in Behaeltnissen mit einem Inhalt von <= 2 l (KN 2208 30 71)
new_classes.definition_en: Blended Scotch Whisky produced by blending one or more single malt Scotch whiskies with one or more single grain Scotch whiskies, presented in containers holding 2 litres or less (CN code 2208 30 71).

coverage_explanation: All key distinguishing criteria are captured: (1) Blended Scotch Whisky type — blending of single malt Scotch whiskies with single grain Scotch whiskies, modelled via BlendedScotchWhiskyQuality and the BlendedScotchWhiskyBlendingProcess; (2) Scottish geographic origin — the blending process occurs in ScotlandSite (already in TBox); (3) container size <= 2 l — modelled via hasPart some SmallContainer. A minor gap (score 0.95 rather than 1.0) is that the exact distinction between 22083071 (<=2 l) and its sibling 22083079 (>2 l) is only partially enforced by the SmallContainer restriction; full mutual exclusion with 22083079 would require a complement restriction on the large-container sibling class, which has not yet been defined in the running TBox.
